items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: Mimika, as you requested, I ordered a fully equipped team to get ready at our branch.

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: Thank you!

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: Though I still don't understand why you had all the Explosive Spears moved to the branch...

options.text: Shouldn't we move them to the heart of the Sea of Flames?

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: What's on your mind exactly, Mimika? It should be time you tell us.

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: Right. I think it would be really tough to carry the heavy Spears through the Sea of Incinero Petals.

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: That's when I came up with the alternative to ground transport: the air!

options.text: The air?

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: Back in the day, my manager Tang Manliu spent a fortune on a freight catapult for our branch. I figured, if we can catapult goods, why not the Spears too?

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: I-I didn't expect that!

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: If we can catapult the Spears at the maximum power, and correct their trajectory while they fly over the Sea of Flames, they should land right at their destination.

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: Won't this trigger immediate explosions upon landing?

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: Rest assured. Mr. Mortefi said an initiation is needed for them to explode.

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: To ensure everything goes smoothly, Mr. Mortefi has calculated the trajectory, and Mr. Woodrow has confirmed that the path won't be affected by the Gravity Storm.

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: Hey, Mimika. The catapult is all set.

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: All right. The rest is yours, {Male=sir;Female=miss}. This time, we will put an end to it all!

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: That spot is like the epicenter of the Gravity Storm. If we just launch the spears right through it... They would likely get thrown off course by the storm.

options.text: So the Tacet Mark is in the gravity storm's center?

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: Hey, Rover, you see that big building? The Tacet Mark core of all these Incinero Petals is right there…

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: I think the only option is to escape from the Gravity Storm and give it a try up close.

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: Hey Rover, remember when you met the Inferno Rider and it whispered “Rush… Till the end...”?

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: Maybe inside that TD, the wishes of the fallen Midnight Rangers still linger.

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: Rover, if you can outrun that TD, those fallen solders may help you… Help you reach the end, the finish line…

options.text: I understand.

items.speaker: Lollo Logistics Courier
items.text: Hey, Mimika. The catapult is all set.

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: All right. The rest is yours, {Male=sir;Female=miss}. This time, we will put an end to it all!

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: {PlayerName}—!

options.text: Mimika, are you alright?

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: We're fine! I saw it! You just transformed into that Inferno Rider and flew all the way into the air, riding on that Gravity Storm!

items.speaker: Woodrow
items.text: A {Male=young man;Female=young lady} transforming into a flaming rider, rushing to the destination with unyielding conviction to charge at the Tacet Mark! Aah... What a beautiful story.

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: Derived from the fighting spirit of past warriors, the Inferno Rider, in its Echo form, delivered a powerful final blow to the Tacet Mark core of those Incinero Petals...

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: Perhaps it's just me, but... I think the fallen Midnight Rangers would be happy to see this.

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: Tang Manliu...

options.text: Is the danger of the Sea of Flames resolved?

items.speaker: Woodrow
items.text: Our observations show that the Tacet Mark core is nearly completely deactivated, so the toxin from the Incinero Petal pollen has almost disappeared.

items.speaker: Woodrow
items.text: The dangerous Gravity Storms that were gathering over the Tacet Mark core have vanished. We can confidently conclude that... Jinzhou is safe now.

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: And it's all thanks to you!

options.text: This is a result of our coordinated efforts.

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: Then please come visit our branch when you're back in Jinzhou. We'll be waiting for you.

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: Like it or not, we've already declined their package. Let's just pretend this order never existed.

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: You didn't ask anyone before sending that rejection, did you?

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: Even if I had, do you seriously think we could've delivered it?

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: Of course! No order is too tough for us to handle! "We Promise, We Deliver!"

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: So why did you even make the promise in the first place? Ugh...

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: You're talking about the Sea of Flames in Guixu Port City. There's no way I'm letting any of our staff go to such a dangerous place!

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: But, but...

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: Besides, who would order anything to a place like that? It's obviously a prank. What was even in the package?

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: Uh... it was some food, medicine, and warm clothes for one person...

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: Think about it! Whoever made the order must be trapped there, starving and freezing. That's why they ordered those things!

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: Even if that's the case, and I'm not saying it is... We wouldn't make much, just barely enough to cover our costs.

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: Listen, if they really needed it, wouldn't they just place another order?

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: What if they can't just place another order?

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: Enough! This is my call to make as manager. Leave it.

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: This isn't a blame game. We tell our customers “We Promise, We Deliver.” Is that all just for show?

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: Why you...!

options.text: Excuse me, are you talking about this "order"?

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: Wait... This is the rejection email I sent out! How did it end up here...?

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: Mimika! You must have sent it to the wrong recipient when you grabbed my touchpad earlier!

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: There we go, it must be fate! Let me deliver it, please!

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: Oh my... I can’t stand this...

options.text: So, was this a misunderstanding?

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: I'm really sorry we wasted your time because of our mistake...

options.text: Yes, this is a waste of my time.
Don't worry, it's not a big deal.
It's all right. I’m idle anyway.

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: Mimika! Come here and apologize to the customer.

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: S-sorry!!

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: Thank you so much for understanding. You are one of those rare, generous individuals.

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: I-idle, is it? Hmm, what a luxury... Ahem! Hehe, that is a relief.

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: Anyway, it was entirely our fault that caused all this...

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: We'll issue a formal apology later, but for now, please allow me to sort out this strange order.

options.text: Sea of Flames... is that a dangerous place?

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: Do you not know about Sea of Flames, {Male=sir;Female=miss}?

options.text: I've been there.
I know.
I don’t know.

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: Your knowledge is truly impressive! In that case, I needn't blabber on about how dangerous that place is.

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: Hm. The flower there is spectacular for sure, but it's lethal at the same time.

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: You really don't know? The Sea of Flames is a vast field of Incinero Petals whose pollen is extremely toxic.

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: Not to mention the horrible TDs! Few have made it out there alive, as far as I know.

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: Now you see how dangerous it is, Mimika?

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: And that's exactly why we're needed!

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: That customer must know that only Lollo Logistics can deliver what he needs there.

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: Just let me go! Please?

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: I said no!

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: But why?!

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: Just take no for an answer... Ugh, she's so stubborn. {Male=Sir;Female=Miss}, could you help me convince her?

options.text: Don't risk your life so lightly.
I agree. Fulfill your promise!

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: You heard it? Those are words of reason! Don't go there!

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: I will go! If I don't, we may as well tell all of our customers that our slogan is a lie.

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: That's right! No matter what, we have to fulfill our orders! That's what we do at Lollo Logistics!

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: {Male=Sir;Female=Miss}! What are you...

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: Now, just let me take it from here!

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: I'll take full responsibility as the courier for this job! Is that fair enough?

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: Hey!!!
Mimika grabs the touchpad, puts on her delivery gear, and hurries away.

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: Damn it! What now... She must have left for the Sea of Flames!

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: Why? Of all places, why there...

options.text: "Of all places"?

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: Sigh... We don't let our employees go near the Sea of Flames. That place is off-limits.

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: The incident dates back to when Lollo Logistics established our first Jinzhou branch.

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: That place got overrun by Incinero Petals, spewing toxic pollen that drove everyone away.

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: And to make things worse, the Center for Wutherological Climate Studies found those Gravity Storms raging in the area. Talk about adding insult to injury.

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: If that pollen ever got swept up into the upper atmosphere by those storms, it would spell disaster for Jinzhou and the surrounding regions.

options.text: That sounds really dangerous...

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: Absolutely. At the time, the researchers figured out that the only way to stop the spread was to deactivate the Tacet Mark core of those flowers.

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: The Huaxu Academy created Explosive Spears to do the job, but the terrain was too dangerous for any regular vehicle to get through.

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: Most of the Tacetite vehicles were tied up at the frontlines with the Midnight Rangers, fighting off the TD Outbreak, so we couldn't get the Spears to the center of the Sea of Flames.

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: That left Lollo Logistics to step in with our spare Tacetite vehicles and deliver them. The plan was for the Midnight Rangers to use them to take out the Tacet Marks, risking their lives in the process.

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: Everyone knew how dangerous the job was, but we took it on anyway—after all, our motto is "We Promise, We Deliver."

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: But... we couldn't get there in time. Our couriers tried their best, but they couldn't deliver the Spears in time.

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: In the end, only one Spear made it through, and even though we fell short, the Midnight Rangers thanked us for our help.

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: They had most of their people escort our couriers to safety, while a few brave souls stayed behind to charge at the Tacet Mark, knowing it could cost them their lives.

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: They passed the Spear along, one after another, trying to get it to the Tacet Mark. But the deadly pollen was too much. In the end, they all ended up Overclocking.

options.text: Then why is there still pollen there?

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: Sigh... The Spear got damaged during transport, so it didn't fully deactivate the Tacet Mark when it hit. The explosion never went off.

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: But, at least the attack weakened the Tacet Mark enough to stop the spread of the Incinero Petals, and the pollen concentration dropped to a level that no longer threatens Jinzhou.

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: Even though no one blamed them, the survivors at Lollo Logistics were wracked with guilt, thinking their mistake cost those brave soldiers their lives.

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: That mission left a permanent scar on our company. After that, we made it a rule that any major order had to be handled by Resonators with combat skills.

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: "We Promise, We Deliver"... It's just easier said than done...

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: Now you see why that place is such a taboo for us. We don't want couriers like Mimika getting mixed up with the Sea of Flames.

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: All right. That's enough blabbering from me. Let’s focus on finding Mimika...

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: Ah, that girl is as stubborn as a mule and never listens to anyone, but she's also known for being ridiculously fast. If I don't head out right now, I might not be able to catch up to her.

options.text: Maybe I can help.

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: No, no, {Male=sir;Female=miss}! How could I bother you with this...

options.text: I am partially responsible for Mimika running off.

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: Eh... But...

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: All right then. Thank you very much for your assistance.

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: Let's search for her on the outskirts of the Sea of Flames first. I've sent you the coordinates.

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: Would you go with me, {Male=sir;Female=miss}?

options.text: (Go with Tang Manliu)
(Go alone)

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: Okay. Then I'll go first. I'll see you there.

items.speaker: Woodrow
items.text: Hey, aren't you that Rover? You awake?

options.text: Professor Woodrow. Why are you here?

items.speaker: Woodrow
items.text: This is the weather observatory in the Port City of Guixu. You passed out during a battle with the Inferno Rider. Some guys from Lollo Logistics brought you here.

options.text: The Inferno Rider?

items.speaker: Woodrow
items.text: Oh, that's the Tacet Discord you just met. It looks like a rider on a motorbike, so I named it the Inferno Rider. A good name, isn't it?

options.text: And that's the TD you put a bounty on?

items.speaker: Woodrow
items.text: Hahaha! That’s right. There’s a reason why we must put it down.

items.speaker: Woodrow
items.text: Our detection shows that the anomalous gravity field here is giving birth to a giant storm, which will sweep the pollen of Incinero Petal to the upper air.

options.text: Just like the crisis years ago.

items.speaker: Woodrow
items.text: So you know about it. That'll save me the explanation.

items.speaker: Woodrow
items.text: Incinero Petal is not a kind of plant, but an animal Tacet Discord. The flower-like structure is actually the end of its limbs.

items.speaker: Woodrow
items.text: To eliminate the Incinero Petals themselves is pointless. To stop the pollen from spreading, we must destroy the Tacet Mark at its core.

items.speaker: Woodrow
items.text: Initially, we were thinking about blowing that thing up...

options.text: ...But the Inferno Rider came along.

items.speaker: Woodrow
items.text: Technically, it didn't "come" to us; it was "discovered". That Inferno Rider seems to have been in the depths of Sea of Flames since that crisis many years ago.

items.speaker: Woodrow
items.text: Launching a direct assault on the Tacet Mark core deep within the Sea of Flames was already a daunting task, and with this Tacet Discord in play, our mission now seems nearly impossible.

options.text: Why don't you leave it to the Midnight Rangers?

items.speaker: Woodrow
items.text: The special properties of the Sea of Flames make it safer for solo missions, so I've posted the wanted posters, hoping to recruit some skilled Resonators to defeat that TD.

options.text: By the way, who rescued me?

items.speaker: Woodrow
items.text: We don't know either. You should ask the guys from Lollo Logistics who brought you back. They're right outside the observatory.

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: Thank goodness you both made it out safe this time. If anything had happened, you'd have not only gotten yourself killed, but also one of our customers!

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: Sob sob...

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: I swear, if anything had happened to you two, I'd never forgive myself.

options.text: Mimika, are you okay?

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: Oh, you're awake!

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: Oh, sob sob... I-I'm sorry, I really am...

options.text: I'm fine. But where have you been, Mimika?

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: Sob, I...

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: Let me explain. While she was tracking down the person who placed the order, the touchpad she grabbed got swept away by the Gravity Storm.

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: She got lost and wandered around until she bumped into me. I managed to get her out of there.

options.text: So that's why the touchpad ended up there...

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: Yeah, it must've been blown there by the Gravity Storm.

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: The Incinero Petals weren't too bad, but I didn’t expect such a powerful Tacet Discord to be lurking there...

options.text: How did you know? Did you rescue me?

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: Oh, was it really you who saved {Male=him;Female=her}?! That's... amazing!

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: Ugh... I wasn't always a manager, you know. I used to be a top courier, and getting people out of danger was just part of the job.

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: Wait, seriously?! I always thought you were just some old guy who coasted through the company long enough to land the manager gig...

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: Hey, watch it...

options.text: Did you witness the crisis from years ago?

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: I dread remembering it, but that TD... I think it was saying... "Charge... till the end..."

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: Back then, the Midnight Rangers who attacked the Tacet Mark core ended up creating a Tacet Field when they Overclocked. That Tacet Discord might've come from that.

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: I was one of the couriers who delivered the Explosive Spears. I saw it all happen.

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: The determination of those soldiers has now become the biggest obstacle to destroying the Tacet Mark. It's tragic.

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: So this crisis... it's kind of tied to Lollo Logistics, isn't it?

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: What are you talking about...

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: I mean, this could be our chance to redeem ourselves!

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: If we can help them neutralize that Tacet Mark core, it could make up for our past mistakes and fulfill the promise we failed to keep!

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: What are you rambling about? No way.

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: Come on, let me take care of this!

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: Stop. Lollo Logistics can't get involved anymore. We need to stick to our business principles.

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: Even if I agreed, headquarters would never approve. It's not profitable and it risks our employees' safety.

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: If we can't stop the spread of the toxic pollen, no place will be safe. Lollo Logistics won't be able to deliver goods to those who need them!

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: Isn't this a huge threat to our brand?!

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: Ugh... I said no, and I meant it!

options.text: What if I can make this profitable for you?

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: Huh? What did you say?

options.text: I plan to defeat the Inferno Rider and claim the bounty.

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: Listen, I've heard about {PlayerName}. If they're involved, then we definitely have a shot at defeating the Inferno Rider!

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: That's a hundred thousand Credits! How many orders do I need to deliver to make that much money...

options.text: But I can't do it alone.

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: If even someone as strong as {PlayerName} can't handle it... Mimika, you should probably give up.

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: How could this be...

options.text: I want to use the bounty to hire Lollo Logistics.

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: Wh-what?!

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: Wait a minute, now it's not just 'not profitable'! Even the headquarters can't say no to this!

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: A hundred thousand Credits... That's a pretty substantial order.

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: That's it! We have to take this order now!

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: Hmm...

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: Alright! We at Lollo Logistics will take the order. We'll do everything we can to support you!

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: Terrific!

options.text: There's still one crucial problem to solve...

items.speaker: Woodrow
items.text: You've brought some fantastic news. Didn't expect Lollo Logistics to get involved.

items.speaker: Woodrow
items.text: With their transportation, our researchers should be able to navigate through the Incinero Petals with ease. This is a huge help.

options.text: But it's not enough to defeat the Inferno Rider.

items.speaker: Woodrow
items.text: Well, if not even a Resonator like you, {PlayerName}, could defeat it...

options.text: That barrier is impenetrable.

items.speaker: Woodrow
items.text: You raise a good point. We still don’t have a way to break that shield.

items.speaker: Woodrow
items.text: If we can't take the Inferno Rider out, we won't stand a chance closing in on the Tacet Mark core.

items.speaker: Unknown (83)
items.text: [A notification rings on Woodrow's touchpad]
Woodrow, I have the results for the issue you asked me to look into.

options.text: ...Mortefi?

items.speaker: Woodrow
items.text: Ah, Mortefi. You finally deign to respond to my calls, eh?

items.speaker: Mortefi
items.text: Sorry for the delay. Even for someone like me, digging up old records takes time. Your request is from years ago, after all.

items.speaker: Mortefi
items.text: Here's the short version: we still have some spare parts from the Explosive Spears used in the last battle, stored in the Port City of Guixu. They should work if we assemble them.

items.speaker: Mortefi
items.text: With such a weapon, you should be able to breach the Inferno Rider's shield and deliver a powerful blow to the Tacet Mark core of the Incinero Petals.

items.speaker: Woodrow
items.text: I'm curious—how do you know so much about what happened back then, being so young?

items.speaker: Mortefi
items.text: I just came across some records while researching Tacetite weapons. The spears were developed in collaboration between Huaxu Academy and the <ano=CSC>Court of Savantae</ano> back then.

items.speaker: Mortefi
items.text: They were obsessed with making weapons with overwhelming firepower, completely disregarding practicality and safety. Typical of those savants.

items.speaker: Mortefi
items.text: The CSC were known for their wild ideas, but the "Orange Box" group was the most outrageous of all.

items.speaker: Mortefi
items.text: According to the records, the spare spears were split into three parts and stored in three different research centers, for security reasons.

items.speaker: Mortefi
items.text: After all these years, I'm unsure if they still work. But if you can find them, I can guide you on how to assemble and recalibrate them.

items.speaker: Woodrow
items.text: We'll arrange for someone to clear a secure path. {PlayerName}, please help us retrieve the spare parts with the Lollo Logistics couriers.

options.text: Sure. I'll inform Lollo Logistics.

items.speaker: Woodrow
items.text: By the way, Mimika, the courier, was looking for you. She's waiting at the Resonance Nexus.

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: Look! Someone is there!

options.text: Hey, are you all right?

items.text: Cough... water, water... Please...

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: Aah, right here. Drink, please.
I... I thought it was the end.

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: Did you place an order with Lollo Logistics?
Yes, I did. I'm Yuni. I didn't expect Lollo Logistics to really make it here. Thank you so much.

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: You're welcome. Maybe I should apologize... for not delivering your order on time.

options.text: Why are you here?

items.speaker: Yuni
items.text: I don't know. I woke up here, surrounded by toxic pollen with no way out. I was starving and parched, barely hanging on.

items.speaker: Yuni
items.text: The Gravity Storm was messing with my signal... I tried calling everyone in my contacts, but only Lollo Logistics' number went through.

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: Hehe, of course it did! We've invested in strengthening our signal so that anyone can place orders with us anytime, anywhere.

items.speaker: Yuni
items.text: I was worried about losing the signal, so I used your automatic ordering service to send out the request. I didn't expect you to actually deliver it here.

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: Absolutely! At Lollo Logistics, we always deliver on our promises, no matter what!

options.text: So, shall we escort you out of here?

items.speaker: Yuni
items.text: No need to trouble yourselves. I can move around now. Thanks so much... I'll never forget your help.

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: Wait, remember to spread the word about Lollo Logistics! We have discounts for new customers, you know!

items.speaker: Yuni
items.text: Haha, I will. Goodbye for now!

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: Great, the order is finally done. It feels good to wrap this job up.

options.text: You honored your slogan. You delivered on your promise.

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: To be honest, I once thought it was no more than a publicity slogan, too. No one would really take each and every order so seriously, right?

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: But I changed my mind when I received a package delivered by Lollo Logistics. It was something my late father left.

options.text: Your father?

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: Yes. He was a Midnight Ranger. I was told at a young age that he passed away at his post, and that his body was not even found.

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: The delivery guy said my father was injured in a battle against the TD Outbreaks. He was trying to protect his friends when he fell into a valley.

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: A Lollo Logistics employee found him, but they could not save him.

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: They told me that until his dying breath, he didn't stop thinking about me, saying that his daughter was about to turn ten, and he... he couldn't even see her one last time.

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: He hoped that Lollo Logistics would deliver the doll he carried with him as a birthday present to his daughter.

options.text: So that was the package you received?

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: Yes, but it was far more complicated. Father passed before he could say the address and identity, and no identifications could be found with him by then.

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: However, couriers from Lollo Logistics didn't give up. They kept asking and investigating while they delivered other goods.

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: Finally, after all those difficulties, they found the girl. That girl was me.

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: But by the time I received this present, I was already over the age of playing dolls. It was my 18th birthday.

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: Lollo Logistics inspired me. I wanted to join and stick to the motto: We Promise, We Deliver!

options.text: You will.
This order is the proof.

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: Hehe, thank you.

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: One order done. Next up, we have a bigger order to deliver!

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: My colleagues and I will go with you to the research centers Mr. Mortefi mentioned. We'll try to find those spear parts and bring them back.

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: I got the coordinates from him. Let's move.

options.text: (Fast Travel to the location)
(Travel to the location yourself)

items.speaker: Brute Male Exile
items.text: Hey! Fork over your cash and food or die!

items.speaker: Brute Female Exile
items.text: Wait, they came from that research center. We can't let them leave alive.

items.speaker: Brute Male Exile
items.text: Is that it? Wasn't gonna let them live anyway, ha.

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: These are the Exile thugs Yunni talked about, {Male=sir;Female=miss}.

options.text: I can tell. They don't look very friendly.

items.speaker: Brute Male Exile
items.text: So you know that bitch. A dye-in-the-wood idiot.

items.speaker: Brute Female Exile
items.text: I only sadfished a little bit, then she fell for it and followed me to the Sea of Flames.

items.speaker: Brute Female Exile
items.text: The plan was to knock her out, then throw her into the Sea of Flames to lure her cohorts out of the darned research center for a perfect wipe out. Didn't see you saving her, though.

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: Ugh, how despicable...

items.speaker: Brute Male Exile
items.text: Despicable? Hahaha, we'll show you real despicable. You've got a Resonator on your side I see, but {Male=he;Female=she} won't stand a chance against us all!

items.speaker: Unknown (83)
items.text: (What a weird wanted poster... I wonder why.)
Hm? Aren’t you that Rover? Are you interested in this poster?

options.text: No. I was just taking a look.
Yes. It's rare to see something like this.

items.text: Stop pretending. I can sense that you’re interested.
A showdown with a wanted criminal always attracts real experts. I knew it! This is the most effective recruitment trick.
Oh, I almost forgot. I am the head of the Center for Wutherological Climate Studies, Huaxu Academy. You can call me Professor Woodrow.

options.text: Did you post it here, Professor?

items.speaker: Woodrow
items.text: Yes. Doesn't it get your adrenaline rushing to see an old poster like this, made with real ink and paper like in the old days? Hahahaha!

items.speaker: Woodrow
items.text: A powerful one like yourself must have imagined this...

items.speaker: Woodrow
items.text: ...A one-on-one showdown with a wanted criminal on a deserted land of death. Then you claim the bounty and leave as people cheer for you. That is the romance of the wild west!

options.text: What? West...?

items.speaker: Unknown (83)
items.text: [A notification rings on Woodrow's touchpad]

items.speaker: Woodrow
items.text: Sorry, I've got a message...

items.speaker: Woodrow
items.text: Yes, it’s me... All right, I see.

items.speaker: Woodrow
items.text: Sorry, got some emergencies back in my lab... If you're interested in this poster, find me at Donglu Research Station in Port City of Guixu.

items.speaker: Woodrow
items.text: All right. {Male=Young man!;Female=Young lady!} Till we meet next time!

items.speaker: Unknown (83)
items.text: (He was unexpectedly friendly... Not what I'd expect from a professor.)

items.speaker: Unknown (83)
items.text: You suddenly receive a a strange piece of mail.

items.speaker: Unknown (83)
items.text: (A mail from... Lollo Logistics?)

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: {PlayerName}, we’re here!

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: Wow... They're scattered everywhere. I can even see some far away.

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: Reaching the <color=Highlight>barrels</color> deep in the Sea of Flames will expose us to toxins for an extended period. Even strong people like you can be impacted...

items.speaker: Mortefi
items.text: Wait a moment. Something seems wrong with these <color=Highlight>barrels</color>.

options.text: What's the problem?

items.speaker: Mortefi
items.text: I checked the records again, and it turns out a lot of these spare barrels are defective. They were actually meant to be destroyed.

items.speaker: Mortefi
items.text: If you were to make an Explosive Spear with defective parts, it could trigger an immediate explosion.

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: An immediate explosion?! W-wouldn't want that...

items.speaker: Mortefi
items.text: That’s why you should test the durability of those barrels on the spot by by physically impacting them. You'll need at least three functional <color=Highlight>barrels</color>.

items.speaker: Mortefi
items.text: Just find the specs and send them over. Then we can search the warehouse for similar barrels to use as substitutes.

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: What do you mean by "physically impacting them"?

options.text: I guess we just attack them with weapons?

items.speaker: Mortefi
items.text: You're right, {PlayerName}. If they were standard <color=Highlight>barrels</color>, they wouldn't break so easily.

items.speaker: Mortefi
items.text: And those <color=Highlight>defective barrels</color>, once destroyed, contain a substance that can help neutralize the toxicity of Incinero Petals. This should allow you to stay in the Sea of Flames a bit longer.

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: Tang Manliu, {PlayerName} is here!

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: I heard you, I heard you! No need to shout. My ears are ringing from the noise.

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: Please excuse us, {Male=sir;Female=miss}. Mimika is always so noisy. She must have caused a lot of trouble for you back in the Port City of Guixu.

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: I didn't. Please, {PlayerName}, say something!

options.text: Mimika was a great help.
Mimika was a trouble maker.
I can't come up with a rating for her yet...

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: Thank you. It was my pleasure!

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: You hear that? I'm quite capable!

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: You must be kidding, aren't you?

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: As I anticipated... I'm so, so sorry, {Male=sir;Female=miss}!

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: Don't be so shy. Feel free to praise me, {PlayerName}!

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: Anyway, I do need to thank you, Mimika.

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: For real? What's with the sudden compliment? It feels so off to hear that from you.

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: Your insistence saved that customer in the Sea of Flames and ensured successful delivery of the Spears. Without you, our customer may have been consumed by TDs.

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: You're right, Mimika. "We Promise, We Deliver"... This should never be a mere slogan. It should be the faith we stick to.

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: Hehe! I know right?!

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: That being said, I must, however, ask you to reflect on your impulsiveness.

options.text: Well said.

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: O-Okay...

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: That's the spirit. You will become an excellent courier one day.

options.text: I have faith in you, Mimika.

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: Thank you Tang Manliu, and thank you {PlayerName}! I will work hard!

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: Finally, on behalf of Lollo Logistics, allow me to express our sincerest gratitude to you, dear customer.

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: Without your help, this crisis would not have been resolved so well.

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: We would probably have to doubt ourselves in regret and shame. It would be a disgrace to our slogan, "We Promise, We Deliver!"

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: Therefore, we thank you... for letting us honor our promise and deliver this overdue order.

options.text: No need to thank me. It's a victory for all of us.

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: You are being modest.

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: The one-hundred-thousand-credit order you placed with us is hereby finished, but since it's your first order, a discount must apply.

items.speaker: Tang Manliu
items.text: The discounted amount will be returned to you as a token of our appreciation, mine especially. Please accept it.

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: Thank you for using our service. "We Promise, We Deliver!"

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: We look forward to seeing you again. Goodbye.

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: Hello! We're back with the <color=Highlight>filter</color>!

items.speaker: Feeble Exile
items.text: Almost thought we wouldn't make it... I'll get the <color=Highlight>filter</color> installed right away!

items.speaker: Feeble Exile
items.text: I can't thank you enough... We would have all died without you.

items.speaker: Feeble Exile
items.text: I know why you're here. Yuni told me all about it, but the spear parts are...

items.speaker: Mimika
items.text: I can guess it. We'll need to solve another puzzle made by those weird geniuses.

items.speaker: Feeble Exile
items.text: Um, actually... When those thugs attacked us, they messed around with the device in the lab, and some... accidents happened at the warehouse, where the spear parts are kept...

options.text: Accidents?

items.speaker: Feeble Exile
items.text: Yes, all the Spear <color=Highlight>barrels</color> were launched into the Sea of Flames, with some landing quite deep inside.

items.speaker: Feeble Exile
items.text: Sigh... I feel ashamed to admit this, but we can't get them out from there...

items.speaker: Feeble Exile
items.text: I guess... You'll have to go get those barrels yourselves.

options.text: Do you have the coordinates?

items.speaker: Feeble Exile
items.text: Yeah, I'll share them with you.
